title: CoTabor AI Settings
subtitle: Manage AI memory backends and external tool integrations

language.label: Language
language.description: Switch the plugin UI and Agent output language

llm.title: Global LLM Configuration
llm.description: Configure your OpenAI-compatible endpoint for CoTabor's planner and executor. Leave blank to use default environment variable settings.

provider.label: Model Provider
provider.customOnlyBaseUrl: To edit Base URL manually, switch Model Provider to Custom first.

openRouter.authSuccess: Authorization successful. Select a model below, then click Save.
openRouter.bannerDesc: No manual API key setup required. Sign in securely with OpenRouter to fetch the model list automatically.

vision.title: Vision Model
vision.description: Used for Cortex visual recovery. It is triggered when the main model cannot locate page elements, and supports UI-TARS, Qwen-VL, and other vision models.
vision.modelSelectPlaceholder: Select a vision model (only image-capable models are shown)

inherit.title: Reuse default model config
inherit.desc: Use the main model above directly without configuring it again.
inherit.noVisionTitle: The current main model does not support image input
inherit.noVisionDesc: Cortex visual recovery requires a vision model that can process images. Turn off Reuse default model config and choose an image-capable model for Vision, such as GPT-4o, Qwen-VL, or UI-TARS.

family.auto: Auto recommend - infer from model name (Recommended)
family.empty: Do not specify - advanced option, may cause visual localization to fail
family.emptyWarning: The official docs mark MIDSCENE_MODEL_FAMILY as required. Without it, Midscene element localization may fail.

notion.title: Notion Memory Backend
notion.desc: Use Notion Database to store AI memory for a seamless experience.

step1.title: Step 1: Connect Notion
step1.noOAuthTitle: OAuth not configured in this build, use manual token below
step1.tokenNote: Go to notion.so/my-integrations to create an Integration and copy the token.

step2.title: Step 2: Set Parent Page
step2.desc: Refresh authorized pages and select a parent page directly. If not found, use search to locate it precisely, or paste the URL manually.
step2.tip1: Click 'Refresh authorized pages' to load pages you currently have access to.
step2.tip2: If the list is too long, search by page title keyword.
step2.tip3: If still not found, open the target page in Notion → top-right … → Connections, add this Integration, then refresh here.
step2.searchPlaceholder: Enter page title keyword for precise search
step2.listNotLoggedIn: Complete authorization to list accessible Notion pages.
step2.listEmpty: No pages found. Try clicking 'Refresh authorized pages' or search by keyword. If the target page is missing, it may not be authorized for this Integration yet.

notion.tip1: If you have already added a 'Connection' manually in Notion, initialization will be faster.
notion.tip2: Clicking 'Build' multiple times is safe — the system reuses existing databases automatically.
notion.tip3: OAuth authorization is the quickest method and requires no manual token generation.

error.noClientId: Fill in the local Notion OAuth Client ID / Client Secret first.
error.noAuth: Complete authorization or enter an Integration Token first
error.noPage: Enter the parent page (Parent Page) URL
error.network: Cannot connect to the Notion API. Check your network, proxy, or VPN and try again.
error.searchNetwork: Cannot connect to the Notion API, so authorized pages cannot be refreshed right now. Check your network, proxy, or VPN; you can still enter the parent page URL manually.

mcp.title: MCP Server Configuration
mcp.desc: MCP (Model Context Protocol) lets you extend CoTabor with external tools and knowledge bases.

builtin.title: 🧩 Built-in MCP (Ready to use)
builtin.jinaDesc: Provides web search and page content fetching. No API key required.
builtin.wikipediaDesc: Provides Wikipedia article search and summary. No API key required.

remote.title: 🌐 Remote MCP Servers
remote.desc: Add remote MCP servers (Streamable HTTP). Supports Cloudflare Workers, Railway, and other cloud deployments.
remote.empty: No MCP servers yet. Click the button below to add one.

form.sseLabel: Use legacy SSE transport (for servers prior to 2025-03)

guide.title: 📖 How to deploy a remote MCP server?
guide.step1: Use create-cloudflare to scaffold a Workers project
guide.step4: Run wrangler deploy and paste the URL above
guide.community: You can also connect official or community MCP servers, such as GitHub Copilot MCP, Brave Search MCP, etc.
